title: Eye gaze hardening: drift-back, cap, reset
description:
User-reported: avatar's eyes go white/roll during click reactions. Investigation reveals THREE eye-rotators with no shared awareness: (1) vrm.lookAt auto-update, (2) expression blend shapes, (3) GazeController manual eye rotation. Plus a dead-code bug: gaze decay path requires mouseX/Y=null but setMousePosition never nulls them, so the mouse-tracked eye offset persists indefinitely after first cursor entry.

Approach (defensive, source-agnostic):

1. Drift back to neutral when idle
   - Add inactivity timer in GazeController. If setMousePosition not called for ~0.5s, treat as no mouse and lerp currentMouseYaw/Pitch toward 0
   - Actually exercises the existing decay path instead of leaving it dead

2. Mouseleave handler in AvatarDisplay
   - On canvas mouseleave, call gaze.clearMousePosition() (new method nulling internal mouseX/Y)
   - Inactivity timer covers this case automatically — mouseleave makes it instant

3. Hard cap on eye rotation
   - In gaze.update, after applying mouse offsets, clamp leftEye/rightEye rotation per axis
   - Suggested ranges: ±0.35 rad pitch (x), ±0.5 rad yaw (y) — past these the pupil rolls out of view
   - Cap AFTER all additions so it catches cumulative rotation from lookAt + expressions + gaze

4. Eye reset during click reactions
   - When clickReaction.isPlaying() is true, lerp eye rotation toward zero instead of mouse target
   - Eyes neutral during flinch — prevents rolling-eyes-during-reaction look
   - Cross-cuts with Bead C (suppress competing animations) — same mechanism

5. Audit vrm.lookAt
   - Log/inspect lookAt.target on VRM load. If auto-set somewhere unexpected, either disable (vrm.lookAt.autoUpdate = false) or align with gaze controller
   - Single source of truth for eye rotation

Edge cases:
- Some VRMs animate eyes via blend shapes only — getNormalizedBoneNode('leftEye') returns null, no harm. Cap doesn't apply to expression-driven eye animation (Bead E covers those weights)
- VRMs with deeply nested eye bones (under head): cap is local rotation, independent of head tilt — correct behavior
- Cap range needs validation against shipped VRMs. Anime models tolerate wider rotations; realistic models break sooner. Start ±0.35 / ±0.5 conservative; expand if too restrictive
- If we don't disable vrm.lookAt, its eye rotation lands before our cap. Cap still catches it — fine

Files: packages/avatar/src/gaze.ts, packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx (mouseleave), packages/avatar/src/scene.ts (lookAt audit at loadVRM)

Tests:
- Unit: feed sequence of mouse positions, then no calls — assert decay applies after inactivity threshold
- Unit: simulate large rotation accumulation — assert cap holds
- E2E (manual): move cursor over canvas, click rapidly, observe eyes never roll
notes: Implementation complete. GazeController hardened with: inactivity timer (drift back to neutral after 0.5s without setMousePosition), clearMousePosition() called on canvas mouseleave, hard caps on eye rotation (±0.35 rad pitch, ±0.5 rad yaw) applied AFTER additive writes so caps catch lookAt/expression-driven rotations too, and setSuppressed(true) during click reactions to keep eyes neutral while body flinches. vrm.lookAt audit confirmed dormant — no target set, autoUpdate is no-op. Cap acts as safety net if a future feature activates lookAt. Awaiting user visual verification: clicking should no longer cause eye-roll/white.

title: BasePose adapts to model rest pose (no T-pose assumption)
description:
[base-pose.ts](packages/avatar/src/base-pose.ts) hardcodes additive offsets (e.g. leftUpperArm.rotation.z += 1.2) assuming every VRM is in T-pose at rest. Models in A-pose or relaxed rest poses get over-rotated, producing awkward idle stances and downstream cascading bugs (e.g. annoyed reaction adds another +0.65 rad on top, wrapping arms behind the back).

This is the root cause of: (a) the user's "idle stance is bad" report on a swapped model; (b) the "arms clap behind the back" on annoyed reaction.

Approach:
- At VRM load, read vrm.humanoid.rawRestPose for each affected bone
- Compute offsets needed to reach a target natural standing pose FROM rest, not assuming rest=zero
- If model is already at or past target (arms already down), set offset to 0
- Recompute on model swap (don't reuse cached offsets)

Edge cases:
- Non-humanoid VRM: no humanoid bones — basePose already no-ops, leave alone
- Unusual rest poses (crossed arms, kneeling): clamp offsets so we never bend AWAY from artist intent
- VRM 0.x vs 1.x: rawRestPose API unified in three-vrm v3, but verify both
- Multiple bones interact (shoulder + upper arm): adjust as a group

Files: packages/avatar/src/base-pose.ts, possibly packages/avatar/src/scene.ts (recompute hook on loadVRM)

Tests:
- Unit: feed mock rest poses (T-pose, A-pose, arms-down), assert resulting offsets target the same final stance
- E2E: load each shipped VRM and visually inspect (manual)
notes: Implementation complete. setVRM now reads each affected bone's authored rest rotation and computes adaptiveOffset = target - rest, clamped to never bend AWAY from artist intent. Per-bone offsets cached at load and applied each frame. Unit tests in base-pose.test.ts cover T-pose / A-pose / past-target / opposite-direction cases. Awaiting visual verification on the model that previously wrapped arms behind back during annoyed reaction.

title: Fix provider connect (test/save/restore round-trip)
description:
User reports cannot connect Aris to a provider. testConnection() implementations are real (1-token pings or endpoint checks), so failure is upstream — likely key persistence, registration, or URL validation.

Hypothesis ranked:
1. Key not persisting: ProviderSettings.tsx only includes apiKey if user typed it; reload reads stored key, but if encryption round-trip fails it's silently dropped
2. Provider stays disabled (enabled=false) after partial save
3. URL validation rejects custom endpoint (HTTPS-only check excludes localhost edge cases)
4. Ollama / LM Studio not running — refused looks like 'failed' with no detail

Investigation steps:
- Reproduce with specific provider, capture exact error
- Surface real error in UI (today: single red 'Failed', no detail). Show provider error body
- Round-trip key encrypt/decrypt across app restart, log if mismatch

Acceptance:
- Each of 6 providers can connect when correctly configured
- Failures show actionable error (network / 401 / wrong URL / model not found)
- Keys survive app restart and decrypt correctly

Edge cases:
- Self-signed certs on custom endpoints (Electron rejects by default). Document; consider opt-in 'ignore cert errors' toggle
- Ollama with no model pulled: /api/tags returns empty array, today returns res.ok so test passes but chat fails. Test should also assert at least one model exists
- LM Studio with model loaded but server off: refused vs OK distinction
- Anthropic vs OpenAI 401 messaging — surface provider error body
- Multiple providers enabled — confirm single source of truth, no race
- CORS for custom endpoints (renderer goes through main via IPC, probably fine — verify)

Files: packages/renderer/src/ProviderSettings.tsx, packages/app/src/ipc-handlers.ts, packages/app/src/key-store.ts, packages/ai-core/src/providers/*

title: Fix click-on-avatar reactions (raycast not firing)
description:
Click-on-avatar reactions are wired end-to-end (canvas raycast → clickReaction.trigger()) but not firing in practice. Likely a raycast or event-propagation issue.

Hypothesis ranked:
1. Canvas has pointer-events: none for dock-overlay reasons (would explain everything; also blocks free-position camera drag)
2. Mesh collection in raycast filtering out SkinnedMesh types
3. VRM bounding box mis-sized; raycast hits empty space inside model
4. Click handler attached to wrong element (parent div vs canvas)

Investigation steps:
- Add temp console.log on click event firing, hit array length, hit object names
- Inspect canvas CSS: pointer-events, z-index, parent overflow
- If pointer-events is the culprit, solve free-position camera drag at the same time

Acceptance:
- Click reactions fire reliably on body, head, arms
- E2E test simulates click at avatar bbox center, asserts reaction fires

Edge cases:
- Dock overlay mode: window may be click-through ('always on top'). Need hover-based 'wake' that disables click-through when cursor enters avatar bbox
- Multiple rapid clicks: escalation logic exists, verify 10s window prunes correctly
- Click during camera transition: should still hit (raycast updates each frame)

Files: packages/avatar/src/click-reaction.ts, packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx

title: Wire-up audit & bug sweep
description: Walk every advertised feature, confirm it fires end-to-end, fix what's dead. Prerequisite to Epic 1 — adding camera modes on top of broken click reactions and provider connect just buries bugs deeper. Two known bugs (click-on-avatar, provider connect) plus a sweep across the rest of the feature surface.

title: Saving provider config silently deactivates active provider
description: initProviderFromConfig calls registry.unregister(id) which clears activeId if the saved provider was active. Provider is re-registered but never re-activated, causing 'No active AI provider set' on chat until Activate is clicked again or app restarts.

title: Quick Install: one-click setup scripts for TTS/STT services
description:
Goal: replace the manual install-step list with a single 'Quick Install' button that runs a platform-appropriate script, removing the friction of pip + huggingface-cli + start-server commands the user otherwise has to type by hand.

User-facing flow:
1. User opens Settings → Voice → TTS Provider, clicks 'Quick Install' on F5-TTS / Sesame CSM / Kokoro / Whisper card
2. Aris asks for an install location (or uses a default under app.getPath('userData')/services/<name>/)
3. Script runs, streaming stdout/stderr to the UI
4. On success the service starts automatically and Aris registers it

Scope:
- Ship platform-specific scripts in packages/app/setup-scripts/<service>/install.{ps1,sh} that automate the install steps currently surfaced as text in install-orchestrator
- New IPC channel install:run-quick-install that spawns the script with the chosen install dir, streams progress events back, returns success/failure
- New ServiceInstallInfo field: hasQuickInstall: boolean, quickInstallScript?: { win32: 'install.ps1', darwin: 'install.sh', linux: 'install.sh' }
- TTSProviderSettings + RepairPanel: render a Quick Install button alongside the existing Setup card when hasQuickInstall is true
- Initial scripts cover: F5-TTS, Sesame CSM, Kokoro, Whisper. Each script: clone repo, set up venv, pip install, pull weights via huggingface-cli, write a 'start.bat' / 'start.sh' shortcut so user can re-launch the server later

Non-goals:
- Bundling Python itself (still requires user has Python 3.10+ on PATH; script checks and surfaces a clear error if missing)
- Auto-restart / process supervision (separate bead — sidecar lifecycle deferred earlier as ARI-oiu)

Replaces the manual-install-only flow shipped under ARI-4ij/yqe/uw8.
close_reason: F5-TTS Quick Install end-to-end vertical slice landed (commit 53fb94e). Pattern proven; Kokoro/Sesame/Whisper scripts split out as ARI-followup.

title: Replace Fish Speech with F5-TTS as primary GPU TTS provider
description:
Fish Speech is CUDA-only on Windows in practice. F5-TTS is MIT-licensed, near-Fish quality on prosody, and runs on AMD/NVIDIA/Apple via PyTorch's DirectML/CUDA/Metal backends. Drops in as a replacement in the slot ARI-xij created.

Code changes:
- @aris/voice: delete fish-speech-provider, add f5tts-provider (HTTP client to a community OpenAI-compatible wrapper, default 127.0.0.1:7860)
- @aris/shared: ServiceName 'fish-speech' → 'f5-tts'
- install-orchestrator + install-manifest: replace entries, update install steps to reference SWivid/F5-TTS
- service-detector: replace detectFishSpeech with detectF5TTS
- uninstall-orchestrator: rename target meta
- RepairPanel SERVICE_DISPLAY + TTSProviderSettings KNOWN_PROVIDERS: rename

Supersedes ARI-xij and ARI-4ij (which shipped Fish Speech in worktree-tts-providers branch but never merged to main).
close_reason: Fish Speech -> F5-TTS swap landed (commit b4fe75c)

title: Suppress competing animations during click reaction
description:
Idle, variations, and gaze controllers run additively while click reaction plays. Damp them while a reaction is active so the reaction reads clearly.

Approach:
- ClickReactionController already has isPlaying() — expose it or mirror as an avatarState flag
- IdleAnimation, IdleVariationManager, GazeController accept a 'damping' multiplier (default 1.0)
- AvatarDisplay frame loop: when click reaction is playing, set dampers to ~0.3
- Smoothly restore to 1.0 over ~0.2s when reaction ends (avoid pop)

Edge cases:
- Other reactions (physics, surprise, gesture) also competing — extend damping pattern there if helpful
- Don't damp expressions or microExpressions — face should still feel alive
- Don't fully zero idle — even 0.0 looks lifeless; 0.3 keeps subtle breathing/blinking

Files: packages/avatar/src/idle-animation.ts, idle-variations.ts, gaze.ts, packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx

title: Location-aware click reactions (zone detection + per-zone primitives)
description:
Today every click on the avatar triggers the same chain (surprised → giggle → annoyed → pushback). Make reactions location-aware: head clicks feel like headpats, shoulder clicks feel like pokes, etc.

Hit-zone detection:
- Coarse: existing Box3.setFromObject ray-vs-bbox cull
- Fine: for a small bone set (head, leftShoulder, rightShoulder, chest, leftHand, rightHand, hips), compute world position. Project each onto click ray. Pick min perpendicular distance, within per-zone radius
- Map closest bone → zone

Zones and primitives:
| Zone | Bones | 1st reaction | Escalation chain |
|------|-------|--------------|------------------|
| head | head | headpat (eyes squint, slight smile, head tips into touch) | blush → flustered "stop" |
| shoulder | leftShoulder, rightShoulder | poke-shoulder (flinch on hit side, glance) | annoyed shrug → push away |
| chest | chest | poke-chest (surprised + arms protect torso) | crossed arms → pushback |
| arm | leftHand, rightHand | poke-arm (jitter on hit-side arm + look) | shake out → away gesture |
| body | fallback | current 'surprised' chain | current chain |

Reaction primitives (NEW, in click-reactions/ directory):
- headpat — affectionate, OPPOSITE valence from current chain. Eyes ~70% closed, mouth corners up via 'happy' 0.4 weight, head tilts into touch (-0.06 rad x, +/-0.04 rad z toward touched side), spine relaxes (0.05 rad x). 0.8s. Shy/content feel
- poke-shoulder — shoulder shrug-up on hit side via clavicle, head turn toward hit, surprised 0.6 weight. Asymmetric L vs R
- poke-chest — torso pulls back (spine -0.08 rad x), arms protect, surprised → angry escalation
- poke-arm — quick jitter on hit-side upper+lower arm, head turn toward point, mild surprise

Escalation:
- Per-zone click counter inside 10s window; zone-local primitive choice
- Global irritation counter (sum across zones) feeds severity multiplier
- Zone counters reset independently — head clicks don't reset shoulder cooldown

Edge cases:
- Bone missing on non-humanoid: zones with no bone don't resolve; fall back to body
- Click between two bones: pick min distance, tie-break head > shoulder > chest > arm
- Per-zone radius depends on avatar size: derive from bbox diagonal at load (small/chibi avatars get smaller radii)
- Front vs back: ray hits back of head with same closeness as front. Acceptable v1; flag for later (depth-aware hit, more expensive)
- aerial camera (top-down): mostly all clicks resolve as headpat. Probably charming
- flat2d (orthographic): math still works; verify with smoke test
- Persona modulation hook for later: shy makes headpat more flustered; tsundere escalates poke faster. Keep API ready (setPersonaModulator)

Depends on:
- D (basePose adapts) — primitives stack on basePose; bad basePose corrupts new reactions
- E (expression weights) — new primitives author at toned-down weights from day one
- A (camera-aware amplitude) — primitives need amplitude scaling baked in before zone-specific authoring

Files:
- New: packages/avatar/src/click-zones.ts (zone definition, bone list, hit detection)
- New: packages/avatar/src/click-reactions/ (headpat.ts, poke-shoulder.ts, poke-chest.ts, poke-arm.ts)
- Modify: packages/avatar/src/click-reaction.ts — accept zone in trigger(), dispatch, manage per-zone counters + global irritation
- Modify: packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx — call resolveZone() in click handler, pass to trigger(zone)
- Tests: zone-detection unit (mock bone world positions), per-primitive bone-modification unit, e2e smoke

title: Camera-aware reaction amplitude
description:
Reactions are tuned for portrait camera (close head/shoulders). At fullbody/room/aerial framing, the same bone deltas read as "no movement" on the visible mesh while the shadow exaggerates them. Make reaction amplitude camera-aware.

User confirmation: turning room off (camera flips back to portrait) made reactions visible. Confirms amplitude scaling is the right fix for fullbody/room mode.

Approach:
- Add setCameraMode(mode) on amplitude-aware controllers (ClickReactionController, PhysicsReactionController, SurpriseAnimationController, IdleAnimation body intensity)
- Per-mode multipliers for bone-rotation deltas:
  - headshot 0.6×
  - chestup 1.0× (baseline)
  - kneeup 1.4×
  - fullbody 1.8×
  - room 1.8×
  - aerial 2.0×
  - flat2d 1.0×
- Expressions (face blend shapes) do NOT scale — face is always at full readable strength
- Per-bone-group weight: head 1.0, body 1.5 at fullbody so torso lean reads clearly even more than head tilt
- AvatarDisplay wires camera-mode-changed event → all amplitude-aware controllers

Edge cases:
- Camera transition mid-reaction: lerp amplitude over the camera transition so in-flight reaction doesn't snap. Sample at start of update each frame
- Don't compound with Bead D's basePose contribution — basePose stays identical regardless of camera mode
- Stack with Bead F clamp — amplitude scaling shouldn't blow past bone-clamp ranges; if it would, the clamp catches it
- aerial / flat2d are part of Epic 1's camera modes — this bead can ship before Epic 1 lands by treating those modes as no-op fallbacks

Files: packages/avatar/src/click-reaction.ts, physics-reactions.ts, surprise-animations.ts, idle-animation.ts (body intensity), packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx

title: Bound expression weights for cross-VRM safety
description:
Click and surprise reaction controllers call setExpression('angry'/'surprised') which slams the blend shape to weight 1.0. Some VRMs have stylized expressions: 'angry' may roll eyes back or whiten them; 'surprised' may use pinpoint pupils. At full weight these look broken on third-party models.

User-reported: "eyes go white/roll" on annoyed/pushback reactions.

Approach:
- Change setExpression calls in reaction controllers to setExpression(name, weight)
- Per-reaction weight table: surprised 0.6, angry 0.5, happy 0.7. Tune by feel against shipped VRMs
- Verify expressions.ts setExpression accepts weight; if not, plumb it through
- Optionally layer two expressions per reaction for nuance (e.g. headpat = happy 0.5 + soft blink) rather than one stylized expression at full weight
- Document this convention in the controller — full-weight expressions on third-party VRMs is a footgun

Edge cases:
- VRM with non-standard expression names (some only define 'happy', 'sad', 'angry', 'surprised', 'relaxed'). Falling back to 'neutral' is fine
- Persona-driven expression style later: shy persona uses softer weights, dramatic persona uses stronger. Keep API ready (multiplier hook)
- Test with at least 2-3 different shipped VRMs

Files: packages/avatar/src/expressions.ts, click-reaction.ts, surprise-animations.ts

title: Soft clamp on cumulative humanoid bone rotations (defense in depth)
description:
Even after Bead D, multiple controllers stacking additively (idle + gesture + click + reaction) can over-rotate bones into broken poses. Add a soft post-callback clamp that limits each humanoid bone's rotation to a sane range relative to its captured rest.

This is defensive — D fixes the root cause, this prevents future regressions where a new controller piles on more rotation.

Approach:
- New module bone-clamp.ts: capture rest rotation per ANIMATED_BONES at setVRM, define per-bone-axis safe ranges (e.g. upperArm.z within ±2.0 rad from rest)
- Hook in AvatarDisplay frame loop after all controllers run, before render
- Use Math.max(min, Math.min(max, rotation)) — soft clamp, no easing required

Edge cases:
- Different ranges per bone (head ±0.6 rad x, upper arm ±2.0 rad z)
- Don't clamp during a held animation that legitimately exceeds the range (rare, would need an opt-out flag per bone)
- Hand bones / fingers: probably skip — they have wide ranges of motion
- VRM rest pose differs per model — clamp ranges are FROM REST, not absolute

Files: new packages/avatar/src/bone-clamp.ts, packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx (frame loop hook)

title: Add Fish Speech entry to install-manifest.json + orchestrator wiring
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Add fish-speech entry to packages/app/src/install-manifest.json with platform-specific weights + runtime URLs (Windows CUDA, macOS Metal, Linux CUDA/ROCm). Extend install-orchestrator.ts to handle the multi-asset download (runtime + weights), verify checksums, and report progress. Reference: existing whisper entry as the template.
close_reason: Fish Speech service plumbed through manifest/orchestrator/detector/uninstaller (commit 1615407). Auto-install via managed venv deferred to a separate bead.

title: Refactor TTS into provider registry (mirror ai-core ProviderRegistry)
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Add TTSProvider interface + TTSRegistry to packages/voice, modeled on packages/ai-core/src/registry.ts. Move existing Kokoro/web-speech code behind the interface. Wire voice-handlers.ts through the registry. Persist active TTS provider ID in settings-store under 'activeTTSProviderId'.

Avoid the bug from ARI-7zl: when re-registering a provider, preserve activeId across the unregister/register cycle.
close_reason: Registry foundation landed in worktree-tts-providers branch (commit 47a6c43)

title: TTS Providers settings tab (mirror AI Providers UI)
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

New tab in Settings: lists Kokoro (CPU badge, default), Fish Speech (GPU badge, install/enable). Test/Activate/Save buttons mirror the AI Providers pattern. Voice picker per-provider. Hardware-class badge surfaces hardware-detect output (e.g. greys out Fish Speech when no GPU). Reference: packages/renderer/src/ProviderSettings.tsx.
close_reason: TTSProviderSettings panel landed in Voice settings tab (commit e0322fe). Voice catalog still uses existing Kokoro picker until ARI-o0r delivers per-provider voice lists.

title: Fish Speech sidecar process lifecycle (start/stop/health)
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Fish Speech runs as a local HTTP server (similar to LM Studio). Need a process manager that spawns it on Activate, stops it on deactivate / app quit, exposes a health endpoint poll, and surfaces logs to the UI for debugging. Reference: any existing sidecar handling in install-orchestrator.ts (whisper service start).
close_reason: Deferred — Fish Speech runs as user-managed Python venv outside Aris's control, same pattern as Kokoro. Re-open under the future auto-install epic when we ship a managed venv.

title: FishSpeechProvider implementation in @aris/voice
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Implement TTSProvider for Fish Speech: synth(text) -> AudioBuffer, supports voice ID selection, supports streaming chunks where the model allows. Talks to the local sidecar HTTP API.
close_reason: FishSpeechProvider HTTP client + auto-detection in initTTSProviders (commit 1fadb1e)

title: Epic: Pluggable TTS provider system (Kokoro CPU + Fish Speech GPU)
description:
Mirror the AI provider registry pattern for TTS so users can pick between voices like they pick between AI providers.

## Goals
- Tag Kokoro as the lightweight CPU option (current default)
- Add self-hosted Fish Speech (Apache-licensed open-source model behind fish.audio) as the GPU option for higher-quality, more expressive speech and optional voice cloning
- Both installable via the existing install-orchestrator pattern (no installer bloat — weights/runtime download on demand when enabled)
- TTS Providers settings tab analogous to AI Providers, with hardware-class labels (CPU / GPU) and a hardware-detection hint to recommend the right one

## Why
User feedback: current Kokoro output sounds robotic. Fish Speech is the best local-runnable upgrade, but it's GPU-heavy (~1.5B params, needs CUDA/Metal/ROCm). Need both options: keep Kokoro for CPU-only / low-VRAM rigs, offer Fish Speech for users with a spare GPU.

## Non-goals (this epic)
- Cloud TTS providers (fish.audio API, ElevenLabs) — separate effort if we ever decide to break local-first
- Bundling weights inside the Aris installer — too heavy

## Architecture sketch
- packages/voice gets a TTSProvider interface + registry (parallel to ai-core's ProviderRegistry)
- install-manifest.json gains fish-speech entry with platform-specific weights/runtime URLs
- install-orchestrator handles fish-speech sidecar lifecycle (start/stop process, health check)
- Renderer settings UI gets TTS Providers tab

title: Verify install/uninstall PowerShell flow
description:
PowerShell install/uninstall works on clean Windows.

Edge cases:
- Non-admin user
- Existing install (upgrade path)
- Mid-uninstall failure (resume? clean?)
- Data export integrity (round-trip into fresh install)

Files: packages/app/src/install-orchestrator.ts, uninstall-orchestrator.ts

title: Establish error surfacing pattern (cross-cutting)
description:
Many features fail silently today. Establish a pattern: every IPC handler returns { ok, error? } shape; UI always shows actionable detail.

Apply during the sweep — fix the worst offenders first (provider connect 'Failed' is exhibit A).

Also decide:
- Logging strategy: console only, or persistent log file in user data dir?
- Where users see runtime errors (toast? sidebar? log viewer?)

Files: packages/app/src/ipc-handlers.ts, all renderer error displays

title: Verify settings persistence across restart
description:
Every setting survives restart.

Edge cases:
- Encrypted vs plaintext separation (keys vs UI prefs)
- Settings file corruption recovery (don't crash on bad JSON)
- Schema version mismatches after update

Files: packages/app/src/settings-store.ts

title: Verify conversation history (load, switch, search)
description:
Chats save, reload per session, support search.

Edge cases:
- Multi-conversation switching mid-stream
- Search performance with large histories
- Truncated/incomplete conversations from crashes
- Encryption round-trip

Files: packages/app/src/conversation-store.ts, packages/renderer/src/ChatPanel.tsx

title: Verify first-launch wizard flow
description:
New install completes wizard without errors.

Edge cases:
- Skipping steps
- Going back
- Provider added during wizard vs settings later — does wizard state survive?
- Aborting mid-wizard (resumable?)

Files: packages/renderer/src/FirstLaunchWizard.tsx

title: Verify password lock end-to-end
description:
Confirm set password → restart → unlock works.

Edge cases:
- Forgot password recovery (recovery flow? wipe-and-reset?)
- Auto-lock on idle (does this exist? should it?)
- Lock during active stream
- Password change while locked

Files: packages/app/src/password-store.ts, packages/renderer/src/LockScreen.tsx

title: IPC + persistence for camera mode
description:
Today only avatar:camera-mode-changed exists (receive-only). Add avatar:set-camera-mode IPC. Persist scene block in persona record. Emit event on persona switch.

Acceptance:
- Selection survives app restart
- Changing persona via tray, IPC, or UI all converge to same scene state

Edge cases:
- Race on startup: avatar may render before persona scene config loads. Need 'scene applied' gate OR render with defaults then transition once config arrives. Pick one (gate = flash; transition = jarring)
- Failed persistence (disk full, encryption error): log and fall back to in-memory; don't crash avatar

Files: packages/app/src/ipc-handlers.ts, packages/app/src/settings-store.ts

title: E2E coverage for camera modes
description:
Playwright specs for camera staging.

Specs:
- Switch through all 8 modes, assert camera transitions complete
- Switch persona, assert scene + camera both applied
- Toggle space independently of camera
- Free-position: drag, save, reload app, verify position persisted
- Non-humanoid VRM smoke test (load non-humanoid model, verify modes don't crash)

Edge cases:
- Tests must cope with 0.75s transition — assert eventual state, not instantaneous
- Snapshot tests on rendered canvas are brittle; assert camera position/target numerically instead

Files: tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts (new)

title: Camera mode + scene UI in PersonaSettings
description:
Add camera mode picker, space toggle, theme picker, and 'save camera position' button when freeposition selected. Per-persona, not global.

Acceptance:
- Visual preview (or thumbnail) per camera mode
- Live update — picking a mode changes the live avatar, no save button required
- Per-persona scoping

Edge cases:
- Preview thumbnails: per-VRM thumbnails are expensive. Either ship static illustrations OR render at first persona-edit and cache
- Free-position UX: how to drag camera? Disambiguate from click-to-react. Hold modifier (Alt) for camera drag, plain click for avatar interaction
- Reset button on free mode: users will get stuck with bad camera otherwise
- Aspect-ratio change: if user resizes dock while previewing, headshot framing should reflow live

Files: packages/renderer/src/components/PersonaSettings.tsx (confirm path)

title: Expand CameraMode to preset registry (8 modes)
description:
Replace 2-mode enum with named registry. Each preset declares: id, position, target, optional fov, projection (perspective/orthographic), followAvatar, allowUserOffset.

Modes: headshot, chestup, kneeup, fullbody, room, aerial, flat2d, freeposition.

Acceptance:
- Switching mode interpolates over TRANSITION_DURATION
- All 8 modes render without clipping for default VRM
- Orthographic mode (flat2d) properly switches camera type, not just FOV

Edge cases:
- VRM proportions vary — presets relative to head bone position, not hardcoded world-space. Read humanoid.getBoneNode('head').getWorldPosition()
- Non-humanoid avatars (no head bone) — fall back to bounding-box thirds. Add humanoid? flag per preset
- Switching ortho ↔ perspective mid-transition — can't lerp projection matrices smoothly. Snap projection at t=0.5, lerp position before/after
- Animate FOV alongside position
- Aspect ratio — dock window often portrait (9:16). Headshot must account for camera.aspect

Files: packages/avatar/src/camera-controller.ts, packages/avatar/src/scene.ts

title: Decouple camera from space (room) config
description:
Today scene.applySpaceConfig() calls cameraController.setMode() — remove that coupling.

Acceptance:
- Toggling space (room) does NOT change camera mode
- Toggling camera mode does NOT toggle space
- Camera=room with space=disabled: render sensible fallback (gradient bg, no walls)

Edge cases:
- Migration: existing users with space=on, no explicit camera, default to fullbody so it looks the same as before
- Aerial only makes sense with a room — pick: auto-enable space OR show empty world with floor grid only. Document choice.

Files: packages/avatar/src/scene.ts (applySpaceConfig), packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx

title: Persona scene config (schema + migration)
description:
Add to persona schema: scene: { cameraMode, spaceEnabled, spaceTheme?, freePosition? }

Acceptance:
- New persona defaults to chestup + space disabled
- Switching persona triggers camera + space change atomically
- freePosition persists user-dragged camera per persona

Edge cases:
- Persona switch mid-transition: queue or snap-to-target before starting new transition. Don't leave camera halfway between two states
- Migration: add scene field to existing personas with defaults. Never throw on missing field
- Schema versioning: bump persona schema version, write one-shot migration in settings-store
- Default ('factory') stage = chestup + no room

Files: packages/shared/persona.ts, packages/app/src/settings-store.ts (or wherever personas live)

title: Camera staging modes + persona scenes
description: Decouple camera from the room toggle. Add multiple framings (headshot, chestup, kneeup, fullbody, room, aerial, flat2d, freeposition). Make camera + scene part of the persona so each persona has their own stage. Foundation for the future 'walking stage' / 'Aris in their own room' direction.

title: Flaky camera-viewer e2e tests in full suite
description: When running the full `pnpm test:e2e` suite, one camera-viewer test consistently flakes (different test each run — observed test 94 'open/close' and test 254 'persist across full app restart'). All 12 camera-viewer tests pass when running `playwright test camera-viewer.spec.ts` in isolation. Likely electron window-timing flake under load. Bumping the existing 5s polls to a longer timeout, or adding a setting reset in beforeEach, would help.

title: Quick Install scripts for Kokoro / Sesame CSM / Whisper
description:
Follow-up to ARI-d7r. The Quick Install runner, IPC plumbing, GPU-backend detection, and progress UI all landed under F5-TTS. Adding the remaining services is now mechanical:

For each of kokoro / sesame-csm / whisper:
1. Drop install.ps1 + install.sh + (if needed) api_server.py under packages/app/resources/install-scripts/<service>/
2. Set hasQuickInstall: true in the corresponding *Info builder in install-orchestrator.ts
3. Smoke test on Windows (PowerShell) and ideally one *nix host

Special considerations per service:
- kokoro: Already has a real FastAPI server upstream (remsky/Kokoro-FastAPI); the script just needs to clone, venv, pip install, run it. No Aris wrapper needed
- sesame-csm: Heavier model (~6-8GB VRAM). Needs the same minimal API wrapper pattern as F5-TTS since upstream doesn't ship a server. Note torchao + DirectML quirk on AMD Windows in the install steps
- whisper: whisper.cpp ships pre-built binaries via release zip — the existing install-orchestrator already handles the download+extract path. Quick Install here is mostly about chaining download → extract → start; less about Python venv setup

title: Desktop-wide cursor tracking for gaze (Aris watches the user)
description:
Today gaze tracks the cursor only when it's over the avatar canvas. Aris is a companion — eyes should follow the user across the entire desktop, not just within the app window. This is a separate feature from the canvas-only mousemove tracking.

Approach:
- Main process polls cursor position via Electron's screen.getCursorScreenPoint() at ~30 fps (setInterval, balance smoothness vs CPU cost)
- Convert global cursor coords → direction relative to the avatar window's screen position
- IPC channel cursor:global-position pushes Direction { yaw, pitch } to renderer
- GazeController accepts global cursor data via new setGlobalCursorDirection(yaw, pitch) — feeds the same currentMouseYaw/Pitch path so existing decay, suppression, and caps still apply
- AvatarDisplay subscribes to the channel and routes to gaze controller

Edge cases:
- Permissions: macOS requires Accessibility permission for global cursor access. Surface a permission prompt + status indicator (similar to other accessibility-gated features). Linux: Wayland may block global cursor entirely, fall back to canvas-only tracking with a notice
- Performance: 30 Hz poll is cheap but not free. Add a config toggle (default ON) so users can disable if they prefer canvas-only tracking
- Multi-monitor: cursor on a different display than Aris — clamp gaze direction so eyes don't roll past their cap (Bead G handles this defensively, but verify behavior feels right)
- Game windows that capture the cursor: getCursorScreenPoint still returns coords, but cursor is invisible to the user. Eyes will still track which is fine — it's the intent (Aris watches where you're focused even when game hides cursor)
- App minimized / not focused: skip polling to save CPU. Resume on focus or window restore
- DPI / display scaling: getCursorScreenPoint returns DIPs; convert to physical pixels relative to avatar window. Test 100%/125%/150%
- Privacy: cursor coords stay in main process and feed only the gaze direction calc — never logged, never stored. Document this in PrivacyPanel / settings UI when adding the toggle
- Aris window dragged: position changes invalidate the relative-direction calc. Either recompute on window:position-changed event, or compute direction in main process where window position is known
- Coordinate convention: avatar faces camera (vrm.scene rotation Math.PI), so yaw/pitch already need negation per Bead G. Make sure the global-cursor path goes through the same negation for consistency

Files:
- packages/app/src/cursor-tracker.ts (new) — main process polling loop + IPC emit
- packages/app/src/main.ts — wire tracker lifecycle (start on ready, stop on quit, suspend when not focused)
- packages/shared/src/types.ts — CursorDirection type
- packages/avatar/src/gaze.ts — setGlobalCursorDirection method + integration
- packages/renderer/src/AvatarDisplay.tsx — subscribe to cursor:global-position, route to gaze
- packages/renderer/src/components/[settings] — toggle in privacy/avatar settings (default ON)
- Tests: e2e smoke (channel exposed, polling can be enabled/disabled); unit (direction calc with mock window/cursor positions)

Depends on:
- ARI-6vw.24 (Bead G — eye gaze hardening) — global-cursor path should leverage the same caps and suppression so it's safe by default

title: Add Sesame CSM as advanced/expressive TTS provider
description:
Sesame CSM (Apache 2.0, 1B base model open) is purpose-built for conversational AI companions with emotional inflection. Heavier setup than F5-TTS so ship it as a second GPU provider users can opt into for the emotional-realism win, especially relevant given Aris's avatar expression/lip-sync system.

Code changes parallel ARI-X1 (the F5-TTS swap):
- New SesameCSMProvider HTTP client targeting a community wrapper (default 127.0.0.1:8000)
- ServiceName += 'sesame-csm', plus install/detect/uninstall plumbing
- TTSProviderSettings catalog gains a third card with a longer setup-instructions block (Sesame needs more steps than F5)

Hardware: same DirectML/CUDA/Metal coverage via PyTorch. Some torchao quantization paths may need a DirectML patch on AMD Windows — note in install steps.
close_reason: Sesame CSM provider + plumbing landed (commit 87cf169). Three-tier TTS lineup now: Kokoro (CPU) / F5-TTS (GPU) / Sesame CSM (GPU advanced).

title: Add CPU/GPU hardware-class metadata to TTS providers
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Each TTSProvider exposes a hardwareClass: 'cpu' | 'gpu' field plus minimum requirements (RAM, VRAM, OS). Settings UI surfaces this as a badge. Used by hardware-detection task to recommend a default.
close_reason: hardwareClass + requirements on TTSProvider, surfaced via tts:list-providers (commit 3544e3d)

title: Voice cloning UX for Fish Speech (sample upload + named voices)
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

Fish Speech supports zero-shot voice cloning from a short sample. Add an 'Add custom voice' flow: user uploads/records a 10-30s clip + names it, voice gets stored locally and shows up in the voice picker. Stretch goal — can land after the rest of the epic.

title: Hardware detection helper (CUDA/Metal/ROCm presence + VRAM)
description:
Part of epic ARI-5ym.

New module (packages/app/src/hardware-detect.ts) that reports: GPU vendor, VRAM, CUDA/Metal/ROCm availability. Used to decide whether to recommend Kokoro (CPU) or Fish Speech (GPU) on first launch and to gate the Fish Speech 'Activate' button when no compatible GPU is present.
close_reason: GPU runtime probe + hardware:gpu-runtime IPC landed (commit f9c6d4e)

title: Verify micro-expressions and surprise animations
description:
Confirm fire at expected rates with proper suppression.

Edge cases:
- Suppression with active gestures
- During lip-sync (face busy)
- During click reaction (escalation in progress)

Files: packages/avatar/src/micro-expressions.ts, surprise-animations.ts

title: Verify idle animations + time-of-day
description:
Confirm persona + time-of-day actually shift idle behavior.

Edge cases:
- Day boundary crossings (3am persona switch)
- Manual override hierarchy (gesture during idle)
- DST / clock change

Files: packages/avatar/src/idle-animation.ts, idle-variations.ts, context-idle.ts

title: Verify lip-sync wiring
description:
Confirm mouth moves when TTS plays.

Note: Web Speech API doesn't expose viseme data. Confirm what feeds lip-sync today (audio amplitude only?). Document the gap; full viseme support depends on local TTS engines (out of this epic).

Files: packages/avatar/src/lip-sync.ts, packages/voice/src/web-speech-tts.ts

title: Verify game detection across scenarios
description:
Confirm foreground game returns correct id.

Edge cases:
- Borderless fullscreen
- Dual monitor
- Game launchers (Steam, Epic, Battle.net) vs the actual game
- Two games at once
- Alt-tab transitions

Files: packages/vision/src/game-detect.ts, packages/app/src/process-scanner.ts

title: Verify dock positioning + system prompt
description:
Confirm each dock side places avatar correctly and sends right system prompt.

Edge cases:
- Multi-monitor (which display anchors?)
- Display scaling 125%/150%
- Monitor unplugged while docked
- Taskbar position (top/bottom/left/right)

Files: packages/app/src/main.ts (dock window positioning), system prompt path

title: Verify persona traits actually affect behavior
description:
Confirm trait change visibly alters idle and response.

Edge cases:
- Switching persona mid-conversation
- Conflicting traits (e.g., shy + extroverted)
- 'Default' vs custom persona

Files: packages/shared/persona.ts, packages/avatar/src/idle-variations.ts, system prompt builder

title: Verify window-shake physics reactions
description:
Confirm shake triggers physics reaction.

Edge cases:
- Shake threshold sane?
- False positives during dock drag
- Shake during reaction (queue or interrupt?)

Files: packages/app/src/main.ts (shake detection), packages/avatar/src/physics-reactions.ts

title: Verify beat reactivity end-to-end
description:
Confirm avatar nods/bobs in time when audio plays.

Verify:
- Source feeding the analyzer: TTS output? mic? system audio?
- Silent state behavior
- Headshot camera mode (cross-ref Epic 1): hip bob invisible — should energy redirect to head?

Files: packages/renderer/src/audio-analyzer.ts, packages/avatar/src/beat-reaction.ts

title: Verify proactive screen reactions
description:
Confirm Aris proactively comments when game starts/changes.

Verify:
- Cooldown survives app restart?
- Personality dial overrides cooldown?
- Two games at once
- Game ends mid-cooldown

Files: packages/app/src/screen-reaction.ts
